meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Students: Overcome No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Overcome no SaaS knowledge with our expert starter kit. Follow this guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first portfolio-ready SaaS app.
content.answer_block: The hurdle of 'no SaaS knowledge' vanishes when you use a pre-architected SaaS starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid boilerplate generation and Claude Code for iterative development, you skip the configuration nightmare, focusing entirely on building high-value features that prove your engineering capability to future employers.
content.intro:
The blank editor stares back, a silent testament to the 'no SaaS knowledge' wall that stops most students dead in their tracks. You know the theory, but the leap from writing snippets to architecting a production-grade SaaS feels like jumping a canyon with no bridge. The frustration is real—the endless rabbit holes of auth, database schema, and payment integration.

But here is the secret: nobody builds from scratch. Professional engineers use starter kits to bypass the boilerplate. With Antigravity and Claude Code, you aren't just writing code; you are orchestrating a system. It is time to stop struggling with configuration and start building your future.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Establish a secure, scalable directory structure using a battle-tested SaaS boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack SaaS application using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include pre-configured authentication, user dashboard, and billing middleware.

steps.title: Configure Database Schemas
steps.description: Define your data models to support multi-tenancy from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS. Include tables for users, organizations, subscriptions, and usage_logs with RLS policies enabled.

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Auth
steps.description: Secure your application routes using industry-standard middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure middleware for Next.js that handles authentication redirects and role-based access control for /admin and /dashboard routes.

steps.title: Integrate Payment Logic
steps.description: Hook up Stripe to handle subscriptions and webhooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler to sync subscription status with the database and update user access levels accordingly.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push your finished project to a global edge network.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file optimized for a high-performance Next.js SaaS application.

pro_tips: Always use Environment Variables for API keys; never hardcode secrets, even in private student repos.
Prioritize 'Time-to-First-Deploy' over feature perfection; a live ugly site beats a perfect local build.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your database queries before you write the UI; it prevents 90% of bugs.
Implement a 'feedback loop' in your UI early so you can iterate based on real user actions.
Focus on one core 'pain point' feature; a SaaS that solves one problem perfectly is worth more than a bloated app.
content.next_steps: Once your app is deployed, focus on building a 'public build log' on X or LinkedIn to document your journey and attract potential users or recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder. The wall of 'no SaaS knowledge' is behind you, and a portfolio of production-ready code is in front of you. Don't let your ideas stay in the draft folder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
